auth.createDesc: Set the email and password that will protect this dashboard.
auth.signInDesc: Sign in to manage your keys, routing, and analytics.
auth.serverError: Can't reach the server. Make sure the backend is running (npm run dev).

keys.pageDesc: Provider credentials and the unified API key your apps connect with.
keys.unifiedDesc: Use this as your OpenAI api_key; it authenticates requests to this proxy.
keys.serverReachError: Can't reach the server on {{baseUrl}}. Make sure the backend is running. npm run dev starts both, and the server logs print under the server prefix.
keys.keylessNote: No API key needed: this provider's free tier is anonymous (rate-limited per IP).
keys.label: Label
keys.noKeys: No provider keys yet. Add one above to start routing.
keys.customDesc: Point at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint: llama.cpp, LM Studio, vLLM, a local Ollama, or a remote gateway. Add each model you want routed; they all share the one endpoint. The API key is optional (most local servers don't need one).

playground.pageDesc: Send a chat completion through the router and see which provider serves it.
playground.placeholder: Type a message… (⏎ to send, ⇧⏎ for newline)
playground.emptyTitle: Send a message to get started.
playground.emptyDesc: Using {{model}}. Switch models in the selector above.

fallback.pageDesc: Pick a routing strategy. In Manual mode you drag to set the order; the other strategies route by live score across reliability, speed and intelligence.

strategy.manualBlurb: Route in the exact order you set below. Drag the handles to reorder. No scoring; the chain is followed top-to-bottom.
strategy.balancedBlurb: Reliability leads (50%), with speed and intelligence weighted equally (25% each). A sensible all-round default.
strategy.smartestBlurb: Prefer the most capable model that still works. Intelligence 55%, reliability 35%, speed 10%.
strategy.fastestBlurb: Prefer the fastest model that still works. Speed 55%, reliability 35%, intelligence 10%.
strategy.reliableBlurb: Maximize success rate above all. Reliability 70%, speed and intelligence 15% each.
strategy.customBlurb: Set your own balance of reliability, speed and intelligence with sliders. Same engine as the presets, just your weights.

fallback.customWeightsDesc: Sliders are independent; shares auto-balance to 100%.
fallback.weightBelowZero: At least one weight must be above zero.
fallback.noModels: No models available. Add API keys on the Keys page first.
fallback.guardrailsHint: Always-on guardrails: free-quota headroom × live rate-limit penalty. Below 1.0 means the model is being held back.
fallback.scoreHint: Final routing score = weighted average of the three axes, multiplied by the guardrails. Higher routes first.
fallback.manualMode: Manual mode: requests follow the order below, top-to-bottom. Drag to reorder.
fallback.scoreMode: Scores update from live traffic. The order below is how requests are routed right now.

analytics.pageDesc: Request volume, latency, token usage, and failures.
analytics.extrapolatedHint: The number shown projects your pace from the last {{span}} of data to a full 30 days.
analytics.realTotalHint: The number shown is your real 30-day total.
analytics.pinnedHint: {{pinned}} of these requests pinned a specific model by name. {{honored}} were served by the pinned model; {{failed}} failed over to a different one. The rest were auto-routed.
analytics.autoHint: All requests in this period were auto-routed; no client pinned a specific model by name.

embeddings.pageDesc: Embeddings fail over within a family only: the same model served by another provider. Vectors from different models are incompatible, so the router never swaps models on you.
